essence: Drives landscape‑scale nature recovery by coordinating projects, advising on biodiversity policy, and supporting habitat restoration.

identity.title: Nature Recovery Officer
identity.summary: Supports the delivery of nature‑recovery strategies by coordinating conservation projects, assessing habitats, advising on land management, and working with communities and stakeholders.

salary.notes: Strategic leads or officers working on major landscape‑scale programmes can exceed £62k-£75k+.

qualifications: Training in ecology, conservation, or environmental management
Knowledge of biodiversity policy and nature‑recovery frameworks
Ability to assess habitats and plan restoration projects
Understanding of land‑use planning, ecosystems, and stakeholder engagement

lifestyle.description: Nature recovery officers work for councils, conservation bodies, environmental agencies, and partnerships delivering landscape‑scale restoration. They coordinate projects, assess habitats, engage with landowners, and help implement nature‑recovery strategies. The role suits individuals who enjoy ecology, planning, collaboration, and long‑term environmental impact.

habits: Advises on land‑use planning and conservation policy
Engages with landowners, communities, and stakeholders
Monitors ecological outcomes and environmental change
